subagent_reports.topic: Opportunity scout: /adv-epic user-facing Epic creation command

sources.label: advance-epics spec (adv_spec)
sources.summary: Epic record fields are typed (id,title,narrative,entries,scope); no ultimate_goal field. CLI namespace read-only & fail-closed. v1 forbids Jira-like fields. Constrains command to encode ultimate goal in narrative and use typed mutation tools only.

sources.label: adv_epic_create tool
sources.summary: Args: epic_id (EPIC_ID_SCHEMA camelCase), title (min1), narrative (min1 required), optional scope_kind/owner_project_id/scope_repos. narrative is the ONLY free-text field; no structural ultimate_goal; no overlap detection in tool layer.

sources.label: adv-proposal command overlap recipe
sources.summary: Canonical overlap pattern: `adv_change_list` -> detect overlapping changes; reuse/reference obvious match, ask only if ambiguous. Also documents adv_epic_promote_shell shell->change path and CLAIM_CONFLICT handling.

sources.label: adv-idea command template
sources.summary: Structural template for a collaborative, no-gate, ADV-state-light command: frontmatter+manifest comment, Command Boundary (Produces / MUST NOT / Gate:None), Boundary-vs-Nearby, Exits table, question-tool loop, convergence check, output. Near-exact shape for /adv-epic.

sources.label: registration drift tests
sources.summary: Test-enforced registration burden: manifest entry + command doc (frontmatter desc EXACT match, 5-14 words, single-line scalar) + README.md table + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md table + docs/cli-surface-matrix.md row (agent-workflow-only). All four must stay in lockstep or CI fails.

sources.label: advance-epics overlap-scan sources
sources.summary: Typed read tools available for the open-work scan the proposal requires; adv_epic_list confirmed returning no active Epics in this project (proposal preflight).

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The proposal is boring-by-construction and low-risk: /adv-epic is a thin collaborative command over already-shipped typed Epic tools. The by-the-book pattern already exists in-repo — adv-idea.md is the structural template (collaborative, Gate:None, no change/task creation) and adv-proposal.md already encodes the exact overlap recipe (adv_change_list -> reuse obvious match, ask only if ambiguous) plus the shell-promotion path. The single material data-model question (ultimate_goal) resolves toward the simpler option: adv_epic_create exposes no ultimate_goal field and advance-epics rq-epicNoJiraClone01 forbids field sprawl, so v1 should encode ultimate goal inside the required narrative rather than add a schema field. No new spec law is required to ship a command-only contract; rq-epicCreateCommand01 is optional and should be deferred unless discovery proves the workflow must be enforced structurally. Registration is mechanical but multi-surface and CI-gated (manifest + 4 doc/test surfaces in lockstep).

validation.notes: No anti-patterns. Reuse existing adv-idea structure + adv-proposal overlap recipe rather than inventing new machinery. Avoid adding ultimate_goal schema field (violates simplicity + brushes rq-epicNoJiraClone01). Overlap decision must remain user-confirmed (heuristics advisory only per proposal Must-Not + P33).

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt now: (1) clone adv-idea.md structure for .opencode/command/adv-epic.md; (2) encode ultimate_goal into narrative (no schema change); (3) reuse adv-proposal.md:41 overlap recipe with adv_epic_list+adv_change_list+adv_backlog_state, surfacing update-vs-new as a question only when overlap ambiguous; (4) register across all 5 CI-gated surfaces (manifest.ts, command doc, README table, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS table, cli-surface-matrix.md row=agent-workflow-only) with frontmatter description 5-14 words. Defer rq-epicCreateCommand01 spec law unless discovery proves enforcement needed. Surface to user: the update-vs-new recommendation default and whether ultimate goal should ever become a structural field (future, not v1).
follow_ups: Discovery decision: command-only vs new spec requirement rq-epicCreateCommand01 (recommend command-only for v1).
Confirm frontmatter description wording lands in 5-14 words AND matches manifest exactly (manifest-doc-drift.test.ts).
cli-surface-matrix.md must get an adv-epic row with agent-workflow-only disposition or cli-surface-matrix.test.ts fails.

sources.label: adv-idea command template
sources.summary: Read-only no-gate collaborative template: frontmatter (name+description) + manifest HTML comment + Command Boundary(Produces/MUST NOT/Gate:None) + Boundary-vs-Nearby + Exits table + question loop + convergence + output. /adv-epic mutates (creates Epic) so MUST NOT clone idea's read-only boundary verbatim; it needs an explicit mutate-after-confirmation boundary like adv-proposal.

sources.label: manifest.ts no-gate entries
sources.summary: All no-gate commands use phase:'pre-implementation', NOT 'utility'. No 'utility' phase exists in manifest. Each entry has scope{creates,reads,modifies,gates}. idea/problem have empty creates/modifies. /adv-epic should set phase:'pre-implementation' and scope.modifies/creates reflecting Epic mutation (not empty).

sources.label: manifest.test.ts hard count assertions
sources.summary: Two coupled assertions: expectedCommands[] array (must add 'adv-epic') AND toHaveLength(26)+test title 'contains all 26 ADV commands' (must become 27 and title updated). Both fail without update. This is the highest-leverage easy-to-miss anchor.

sources.label: advance-epics-assets.test.ts
sources.summary: Spec-doc mirror test (lines 31-44) is an INCLUSION list of rq-ids in docs/specs/advance-epics.md; adding rq-epicCreateCommand01 there + a new assertion anchors it. Command-context test (199-228) checks token presence per command file — NOT auto-extended to adv-epic; design must ADD an adv-epic command-contract assertion (Ultimate Goal, overlap, typed-tool tokens) to satisfy AC8 RED.

sources.label: cli-surface-matrix.md + .test.ts
sources.summary: All Epic tools already rowed (adv_epic_create=no-cli-dangerous etc). /adv-epic command row absent — must add agent-workflow-only row or cli-surface-matrix.test.ts fails. C1/OOS1 satisfied: no bin/adv epic create planned; matrix already keeps Epic namespace read-only for list.

sources.label: manifest-doc-drift surfaces
sources.summary: Drift test requires exact-description lockstep across: command frontmatter, manifest.description, README table, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS table(s). ADV_INSTRUCTIONS has a Collaborative-style table(33-34) AND a description table(119-120) AND a Command-only execution-mode list(988) — adv-epic must be added to all relevant rows. SETUP.md table(1341-1342) also mirrors descriptions.

sources.label: advance-epics spec constraints
sources.summary: narrative is only free-text field; no ultimate_goal schema field (confirms C2 — encode goal in narrative). rq-epicNoJiraClone01 forbids field sprawl. rq-epicCliList01.4 keeps CLI namespace read-only (confirms C1). New rq-epicCreateCommand01 must not contradict these.

sources.label: discovery opportunity-scout report (prior)
sources.summary: Prior scout already validated boring approach: clone idea structure, encode goal in narrative, reuse adv-proposal overlap recipe, register across CI-gated surfaces. This design-phase scout confirms + adds manifest phase/count/test-anchor specifics it did not enumerate.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Draft design is boring-by-construction and low-risk; it correctly reuses adv-idea as structural template, encodes ultimate goal in narrative (no schema field, honoring C2 + rq-epicNoJiraClone01), reuses adv-proposal overlap recipe, and uses typed Epic tools only. Four concrete deviations/risks need correction before prep: (1) Design says phase:'utility' or equivalent — NO 'utility' phase exists in manifest.ts; every no-gate command uses phase:'pre-implementation'. Use that to avoid inventing a phase value (simpler, proven). (2) Modeling on adv-idea risks copying its read-only MUST-NOT boundary; /adv-epic MUST mutate (adv_epic_create) after confirmation, so its Command Boundary and manifest scope.creates/modifies must reflect Epic mutation, unlike idea/problem's empty scope. (3) manifest.test.ts has a hard count assertion (toHaveLength(26) + 'contains all 26 ADV commands' title at lines 28-63) that the design's task-5 list omits explicitly — both the array and the count/title must change to 27. (4) advance-epics-assets.test.ts command-context block (199-228) is NOT auto-extended to adv-epic; AC8 RED requires the design to ADD an adv-epic command-contract assertion (Ultimate Goal / overlap-decision / typed-tool tokens) plus add rq-epicCreateCommand01 to the spec-doc mirror inclusion list (31-44). The spec law rq-epicCreateCommand01 (AC7) is warranted here because AC8 demands a failing-without/passing-with anchor; command-only prose alone has no structural test hook unless the asset test asserts the command tokens. No simpler equivalent avoids the multi-surface registration — it is mechanical lockstep enforced by drift tests, not accidental complexity.

validation.notes: No anti-patterns. Boring approach confirmed. Corrections are precision fixes to the draft (phase value, mutate-boundary, exact test anchors), not architectural redirection. C1/C2/C6/C7 and OOS1/OOS2 all consistent with current repo state. Defer of getToolSurface() warrant omission to ag-Adj91lPD is correct and unrelated to this scope.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt draft with 4 precision corrections: (1) set manifest phase:'pre-implementation' (not 'utility'); (2) author /adv-epic Command Boundary + manifest scope to reflect Epic MUTATION after user confirmation (do NOT inherit adv-idea's read-only MUST-NOT); (3) explicitly update manifest.test.ts count 26->27, title, and expectedCommands[] (highest-miss-risk anchor); (4) extend advance-epics-assets.test.ts with an adv-epic command-contract assertion (Ultimate Goal + overlap-decision + typed-Epic-tool tokens) and add rq-epicCreateCommand01 to the docs/specs/advance-epics.md mirror inclusion list — this is what gives AC8 its RED/GREEN teeth. Keep rq-epicCreateCommand01 minimal: goal-first creation + evidence-backed overlap decision, no new fields. Register across README.md, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md (all 3 relevant rows incl. line 988 command-only list), SETUP.md, cli-surface-matrix.md(agent-workflow-only). Frontmatter description 5-14 words, exact-match manifest.description.
follow_ups: TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION anchors were present in packet; all identity anchors present, no packet defect.
Confirm exact frontmatter description wording lands 5-14 words AND identical in manifest, README, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS(x2), SETUP, command file (manifest-doc-drift.test.ts).

Verify whether ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md line 988 'Command-only' execution-mode list must include adv-epic (it lists command-only commands; adv-epic is command-only-with-mutation — decide row placement during prep).
token-budgets.json (.opencode/token-budgets.json) tracks per-command line budgets for idea/problem; check if adv-epic.md needs an entry to avoid a budget-drift failure.

sources.label: advance-epics spec (live, adv_spec)
sources.summary: 15 requirements. No existing requirement governs a user-facing Epic-creation command; rq-epicCliList01 mandates CLI Epic namespace stays read-only (no create/update/delete/archive). adv_epic_create requires narrative (no ultimate_goal field exists). Design's rq-epicCreateCommand01 fills a real gap and does not contradict any law.

sources.label: plugin/src/manifest.ts
sources.summary: Phase union includes 'pre-implementation' and 'utility'. adv-idea/adv-problem are pre-implementation, requiresChangeId:false, scope.modifies:[]. CommandScope.modifies is string[] (free-form), so modifies:['epic'] is type-valid. Both phase choices are schema-legal; agreement AC2 explicitly allows either.

sources.label: .opencode/command/adv-idea.md
sources.summary: Confirms the no-gate collaborative command pattern the design reuses. adv-idea is explicitly read-only ('× MUST NOT: create change'). Design correctly diverges: /adv-epic mutates after confirmation, so it must NOT clone adv-idea's read-only boundary wording — design Key Decision 3 captures this.

sources.label: plugin/src/manifest.test.ts
sources.summary: Two coupled assertions: expectedCommands list (lines 40-56) AND toHaveLength(26) (line 62). Both must move to 27. Design says '26->27' but lists it as a single 'list' edit; the dual update point is implicit, not explicit.

sources.label: plugin/src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts + cli-surface-matrix.test.ts
sources.summary: Drift test enforces exact frontmatter↔manifest description match; CLI matrix test requires every command .md to have a disposition row. Confirms design DDC3/DDC4 and the 'agent-workflow-only' matrix-row plan are backed by real auto-enforcing tests.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is the boring, by-the-book path: a command-file workflow layered over existing typed Epic MCP tools, with mutation authority unchanged. It correctly avoids an ultimate_goal schema migration (encodes goal in required narrative), preserves all Epic invariants (optional membership, advisory order, one-Epic-per-change, product scope, no-Jira-clone), and keeps the CLI Epic namespace read-only per rq-epicCliList01. Phase choice 'pre-implementation' is schema-legal and agreement-permitted; the mutation boundary (mutate-after-confirm, unlike read-only adv-idea) is explicitly modeled. Drift surfaces (manifest count+list, doc-drift, CLI matrix, asset tests, spec mirror) map to real auto-enforcing tests. The only gap is a minor test-update precision issue: manifest.test.ts has TWO coupled edit points (expectedCommands array + toHaveLength) that the design references only as a single 'list' change.

validation.notes: No spec-law conflict, no security/release blocker, no materially simpler equivalent overlooked. One CAUTION: manifest.test.ts requires updating both the expectedCommands array (lines 40-56) and toHaveLength(26)->27 (line 62); a count-only or list-only edit fails. Minor, fully recoverable in prep. Secondary CAUTION (already tracked, not this change's scope): contract warrant tags for Epic tools fail because getToolSurface() omits epicTools (agenda ag-Adj91lPD); agreement correctly avoids Epic tool warrant tags.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED. Proceed to prep. The command-over-typed-tools architecture is the simplest correct approach and contradicts no advance-epics requirement. During prep, make the manifest.test.ts edit explicit: update BOTH the expectedCommands array (add 'adv-epic') AND toHaveLength(26)->27, plus the title 'creates N commands' string if present. Keep phase as 'pre-implementation' (agreement-permitted, schema-legal) and ensure manifest scope.modifies includes 'epic' so the mutation boundary is not misrepresented as read-only. No alternative (new utility phase, ultimate_goal schema field, CLI mutation verb) is warranted — each is explicitly out of scope and would add unjustified complexity.
follow_ups: Prep: split the manifest.test.ts update into its two coupled edit points (expectedCommands array + toHaveLength) to avoid a partial-edit test failure.
Confirm during prep whether ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md and .opencode/token-budgets.json enumerate per-command entries requiring an adv-epic row (design flagged as 'surfaced/partial').

changes_made.summary: Added `requiresChangeId: false` to the YAML frontmatter so AC1 is satisfied structurally, not only through the manifest comment.

changes_made.summary: Tightened the `/adv-epic` asset test to assert name, exact manifest-matching description, and `requiresChangeId: false` inside YAML frontmatter.
changes_made.verification: `pnpm exec vitest run src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts src/manifest.test.ts src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts` passed: 4 files, 100 tests.

tests_run: pnpm exec vitest run src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts src/manifest.test.ts src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts
verification.evidence: Targeted acceptance-review verification passed in plugin/: manifest.test.ts 40 tests, manifest-doc-drift.test.ts 17 tests, cli-surface-matrix.test.ts 2 tests, advance-epics-assets.test.ts 41 tests; total 4 files / 100 tests passed.

risks: Reviewer ran targeted command/manifest/doc tests only; prior implementation evidence covers schemas:check and strict validation.
required_main_agent_actions: Tool schema rejected caller-requested scope_key `acceptance-review`; report submitted with canonical independent review scope_key `review:acceptance`.

consumer_warnings.message: Initial report submission failed because current report schema only accepts change-scope keys matching `review:acceptance` for review summaries, not `acceptance-review`.

subagent_reports.summary: Harden scanners passed. No blocker/high/medium findings. Token budget baseline cleanup fixed and verified. One low informational docs-drift suggestion remains: README/SETUP/CLI table row tests are not enforced, matching existing command-doc pattern and nonblocking.

findings.summary: README.md, SETUP.md, and docs/cli-surface-matrix.md `/adv-epic` rows are not directly test-enforced; this matches the existing command documentation pattern and manifest/doc drift surfaces remain covered.

evidence.label: scanner evidence
evidence.summary: Low informational suggestion only; no release blocker/high finding.

wisdom.content: `advance-epics-assets.test.ts` command-boundary assertions should allow canonical bold command docs (`**Gate:** None`) rather than only plain `Gate: None`; command files commonly use bold labels in boundary sections.

Manifest voice-standard strong-verb allowlist does not include `Create`; new command descriptions must start with an allowed verb such as `Gather`, and exact wording must be propagated to command frontmatter and command tables to satisfy manifest-doc-drift tests.

proposal.approval_evidence: Proposal approved by command-as-approval: user invoked `/adv-discover addEpicCommand` while proposal checkpoint pending after wayfinder showed `/adv-discover addEpicCommand`.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery completed after user approved acceptance/success criteria in chat: `approve`. Agreement persisted and ChangeContract minted. Epic tool warrant-surface omission tracked separately as agenda ag-Adj91lPD.

design.approval_evidence: Design validated with one non-blocking caution. No unresolved conflict, no user-value tradeoff, no contract-compromise risk. Proceeding to prep per auto-pass rule.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep plan in chat: `approve`. Task graph has 3 tasks with contract refs and TDD intent.

execution.approval_evidence: All 3 planned tasks completed with checkpoints. Final targeted verification passed: `pnpm exec vitest run src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts src/manifest.test.ts src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts` (100 tests), `pnpm run schemas:check`, and `adv_change_validate strict:true` (expected NO_DELTAS warning only).

acceptance.approval_evidence: User invoked `/adv-harden addEpicCommand` while acceptance checkpoint was pending after acceptance summary. Per Tier A command-as-approval, this accepts delivered work. Acceptance proof persisted before approval: executive summary hash f8bb595226f87569f4e180c88c4e428e5578ff49d856624070f8ecbc2a434839, contract review matrix 29 rows with 0 failing rows, reviewer READY with 0 findings, targeted tests passed 100 tests, preview URL not_applicable.

items.text: Users can invoke `/adv-epic` to create an Epic through one collaborative, goal-first workflow.
Epic creation never proceeds until the user has confirmed the Epic's ultimate goal.
Overlapping open Epics/changes/backlog work are surfaced before mutation, with neutral evidence and explicit user choice.
Valid Epics can be created with zero initial roadmap entries.
`.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` exists with frontmatter `name: adv-epic`, exact manifest-matching description, and `requiresChangeId: false`.
`plugin/src/manifest.ts` registers `adv-epic` as no-gate, `requiresChangeId: false`, `phase: "utility"` or equivalent no-gate initiative-planning phase.
`/adv-epic` command contract requires an explicit `Ultimate Goal` before Epic creation mutation may run.
`/adv-epic` command contract requires related-work checks using Epic/change/backlog reads before creation.
If plausible overlap exists, command asks user to choose update/clarify existing vs create new before mutation.
Command contract states initial shell/change entries are optional; shell/link additions use typed Epic tools only.
`advance-epics` spec gains a requirement covering `/adv-epic` goal-first creation and overlap decision behavior.
Tests fail without command/manifest/docs/spec anchors and pass after implementation.
Do not add `bin/adv epic create` or any CLI mutation verb.
Do not add a separate structural `ultimate_goal` field to the Epic schema in this change; encode the ultimate goal in the command-required narrative structure.
Do not make initial roadmap entries mandatory.
Do not make Epic membership mandatory for all ADV changes.
Do not treat Epic order as a gate/task blocker.
Do not bypass typed Epic MCP tools or read ADV state files directly.
Keep overlap detection evidence-backed: typed Epic/change/backlog reads plus user confirmation; heuristics may only rank/summarize evidence.
CLI mutation command such as `bin/adv epic create`.
Epic schema migration for a new `ultimate_goal` field.
Mandatory shell/change entries at Epic creation time.
Jira-like assignee, estimate, sprint, board, or ownership workflows.
Automatic implementation change/task creation from every shell entry.
Fixing the pre-existing Epic tool warrant-surface omission; tracked separately as agenda `ag-Adj91lPD`.
Do not create a new Epic when a clearly overlapping open Epic exists without asking the user to choose update/clarify existing vs create new.
Do not present neutral overlap evidence as a hidden recommendation; user chose neutral evidence, not a default recommendation.
Do not weaken existing product/cross-project Epic guidance.
Do not complete discovery based on archived-change scan success; archived scan degraded due `adv_change_list includeArchived:true` timeout and is not required for this scope.

rows.evidence: `/adv-epic` command file exists and is registered in manifest/docs. Reviewer verdict READY; targeted test suite passed 100 tests.
`.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` requires explicit Ultimate Goal confirmation before create/update mutation.
rows.notes: Goal confirmation is command contract requirement.

rows.evidence: Command contract requires related-work scan and overlap decision before mutation; asset tests cover overlap handling.
Command contract states initial entries are optional and `adv_epic_create` may run without shell/change entries.

`.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` frontmatter includes `name: adv-epic`, `description: Gather Epic goals before typed creation`, and `requiresChangeId: false`; `advance-epics-assets.test.ts` asserts these; targeted suite passed.
`plugin/src/manifest.ts` registers `adv-epic` with `requiresChangeId:false`, no gate, and `phase:"pre-implementation"`; manifest tests passed.
Command contract includes explicit Ultimate Goal checkpoint before any Epic mutation; asset tests passed.
Command contract requires `adv_epic_list`/`adv_epic_show`, `adv_change_list`, and backlog read before creation; asset tests passed.
Command contract requires user choice to update/clarify existing vs create new when plausible overlap exists; asset tests passed.
Command contract states initial shell/change entries are optional and shell/link additions use typed Epic tools only; asset tests passed.
`advance-epics` spec and markdown mirror add `rq-epicCreateCommand01`; spec/docs asset tests and schemas check passed.
RED/GREEN task evidence recorded for missing command/manifest/docs rows; final targeted suite passed 100 tests after implementation/remediation.
No `bin/adv epic create` or CLI mutation added; CLI surface matrix row is `agent-workflow-only`; CLI matrix test passed.
No Epic schema field `ultimate_goal` added; goal is encoded in command-required narrative structure.
Command contract explicitly allows creating Epic with zero initial entries.
No change made requiring Epic membership for all ADV changes; existing optional-membership guidance preserved.
No command/spec/doc language makes Epic order a gate or task blocker; existing advisory-order guidance preserved.
Command contract uses typed Epic MCP tools for mutations and typed reads for state; no direct ADV state file reads introduced.
Overlap scan is evidence-backed via typed Epic/change/backlog reads; command text treats heuristics as neutral summarization/ranking only.
Command contract requires overlap decision before creating a new Epic when plausible existing open work overlaps.
Command contract presents overlap evidence neutrally and asks user to choose; no default recommendation encoded.
Product/cross-project Epic guidance in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS remains present; `/adv-epic` row only adds command surface.
Discovery degraded archived-change scan was not used as completion proof; implementation scope relies on active typed reads/command contract/tests.
No CLI mutation command added; CLI surface matrix remains agent-workflow-only for `/adv-epic`.
No Epic schema migration for `ultimate_goal`.
Command allows zero initial entries and does not require shell/change entries.
No assignee/estimate/sprint/board/ownership workflow fields or docs added.
No automatic implementation change/task creation from shell entries added.
Epic tool warrant-surface omission remains tracked separately as agenda `ag-Adj91lPD`; this change avoided requiring that fix.

acceptanceCriteria: `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` exists with frontmatter `name: adv-epic`, exact manifest-matching description, and `requiresChangeId: false`.
`plugin/src/manifest.ts` registers `adv-epic` as no-gate, `requiresChangeId: false`, `phase: "utility"` or equivalent no-gate initiative-planning phase.
`/adv-epic` command contract requires an explicit `Ultimate Goal` before Epic creation mutation may run.
`/adv-epic` command contract requires related-work checks using Epic/change/backlog reads before creation.
If plausible overlap exists, command asks user to choose update/clarify existing vs create new before mutation.
Command contract states initial shell/change entries are optional; shell/link additions use typed Epic tools only.
`advance-epics` spec gains a requirement covering `/adv-epic` goal-first creation and overlap decision behavior.
Tests fail without command/manifest/docs/spec anchors and pass after implementation.

documents.proposal:
# Add epic command

## Problem
ADV has typed Epic tools (`adv_epic_create`, `adv_epic_add_shell`, `adv_epic_link_change`, and related membership tools), but no user-facing `/adv-epic` command contract for creating Epics collaboratively. Users need a guided Epic-creation workflow that forces an explicit ultimate goal, checks for related open Epics and changes, and asks whether to update an overlapping Epic or create a new one before mutating Epic state.

## User Outcomes
- A user can start Epic creation with `/adv-epic` and be guided through the information needed to create a useful initiative container.
- The user and ADV agree on the Epic’s ultimate goal before any Epic state is created.
- ADV checks for existing overlapping Epics, open ADV changes, and relevant backlog/roadmap work before creating a new Epic.
- When overlap exists, the user gets a clear choice to update/clarify the existing Epic or create a separate new Epic.
- Initial Epic roadmap entries are captured as existing linked changes or lightweight shell entries when useful, without making entries or Epic membership mandatory.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Add a `/adv-epic` command contract under `.opencode/command/`.
- Register `adv-epic` in `plugin/src/manifest.ts` as an Epic/initiative-planning command with no gate ownership.
- Define required collaborative checks before Epic creation: ultimate goal, narrative context, scope kind, related open work, overlap decision, and optional initial roadmap entries.
- Define overlap behavior: when existing open Epic overlap is found, ask whether to update/clarify the existing Epic or create a new Epic; present evidence neutrally without forcing a default recommendation.
- Require use of typed Epic tools for mutations (`adv_epic_create`, `adv_epic_add_shell`, `adv_epic_link_change`, `adv_epic_update`) rather than direct state edits.
- Add/update tests for command presence, manifest registration, overlap-check instructions, ultimate-goal requirement, and Epic-rule guardrails.
- Add an `advance-epics` spec requirement for the user-facing Epic creation command contract.

### Out of Scope
- Implementing a CLI mutation command such as `bin/adv epic create`.
- Adding a separate structural `ultimate_goal` field to the Epic schema in this change.
- Making initial Epic entries mandatory.
- Making Epic membership mandatory for all ADV changes.
- Adding Jira-like planning fields such as assignees, estimates, sprints, or boards.
- Automatically creating implementation changes/tasks from every shell entry.

### Must Not
- Must not create a new Epic when a clearly overlapping open Epic exists without asking the user to choose update/clarify vs new.
- Must not bypass typed Epic MCP tools or read ADV state files directly.
- Must not treat Epic order as a gate/task blocker.
- Must not use heuristics as final authority for Epic overlap; evidence must come from typed Epic/change/backlog reads and user confirmation.

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist
| Step | Result | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Skill Discovery | PASS | Loaded `customize-opencode`, `adv-instructions-audit`, and `adv-agent-tool-contracts`; no reviewed skill gap requiring new skill. Pending-review scan found only an instructional mention, not pending skill metadata. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | `docs/*-prep.md` files exist, but none directly cover `/adv-epic`; new finding: command additions are covered by manifest/doc drift tests and CLI matrix tests. |
| Conflict Scan | PASS with tool degradation | `adv_change_validate` passed; NO_TASKS/NO_DELTAS are expected pre-planning. `adv_epic_list` returned no active Epics. `adv_change_list includeArchived:true` timed out twice; active recency scan from proposal found adjacent command changes but no exact `/adv-epic` overlap. `adv_agenda_list` surfaced `Fix Epic cross-project guidance`, relevant for discovery awareness. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Edge cases recorded below. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Open design questions annotated below. |
| Draft Spec Delta Shapes | PASS | Draft `rq-epicCreateCommand01` recorded below. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Similar command surfaces and drift tests found. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Boring-by-construction command-only workflow using existing Epic tools; no external solution needed. |
| Completeness Verification | PASS | Problem and solution scope bounded to command contract + manifest/test/doc/spec surfaces. |

### Skills Considered
- `customize-opencode`: matched because this change adds an OpenCode command file and manifest surface.
- `adv-instructions-audit`: matched because command/instruction prose needs executable anchors.
- `adv-agent-tool-contracts`: partially matched because command packets and typed tool usage must stay aligned; no new tool schema planned.
- `adv-opportunity-scout`: executed through `adv-researcher`; report submitted successfully.

### Extends
- No prior research pack directly covers `/adv-epic`.
- `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md` provides an existing command disposition contract; new finding: `/adv-epic` should be `agent-workflow-only`, not CLI mutation.
- `plugin/src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` provides exact manifest/frontmatter drift enforcement; new finding: adding `/adv-epic` requires exact description alignment.

### Conflict Scan
- Active Epics: none found by `adv_epic_list`.
- Target validation: passed; warnings `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS` are expected before planning.
- Agenda: `Fix Epic cross-project guidance` is related and should be considered during design so `/adv-epic` does not regress product/cross-project Epic wording.
- Backlog: no roadmap item directly named `/adv-epic`; high-priority backlog includes unrelated Temporal/ADV issues.
- Archived change scan: attempted through `adv_change_list includeArchived:true`; timed out despite Temporal diagnosis reporting healthy. Treat as degraded discovery coverage, not a blocking conflict.

### Current State
- `.opencode/command/` has no `adv-epic.md`.
- `plugin/src/manifest.ts` has no `adv-epic` entry.
- `adv_epic_create` requires `title` and `narrative`, where `narrative` is the existing place to encode the initiative goal.
- `adv_epic_add_shell` supports optional lightweight future entries with `title` + `success_hint`.
- `adv_epic_link_change` supports linking existing ADV changes with required `link_evidence` and target-path routing.
- `advance-epics` spec requires optional membership, one Epic per change in v1, advisory order, product scope metadata, and no Jira-like fields.
- README, SETUP, `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md`, manifest tests, manifest-doc-drift tests, and Epic asset tests are current drift surfaces for adding a command.

### Edge Cases
1. Overlap ambiguity:
   - Existing Epic has similar title but different ultimate goal.
   - Existing Epic has different title but same objective/scope.
2. Partial creation:
   - `adv_epic_create` succeeds but adding a shell fails.
   - Cross-project `adv_epic_link_change` target path is unreachable after current-project Epic creation.
3. Empty initial roadmap:
   - User has a valid initiative goal but no immediate child changes.
   - User supplies vague shell entries without success hints.
4. Product scope:
   - User asks for a product-wide Epic but only current repo is configured/reachable.
   - Some product repos are optional/non-required and should not block current Epic creation.

### Open Design Questions
- Command scope surface: trust model agent-owned; blast radius command drift/tests; alternatives command-only vs CLI mutation. Recommendation: command-only, agent-workflow-only.
- Ultimate goal persistence: trust model agent-owned; blast radius schema churn and compatibility; alternatives new `ultimate_goal` field vs encode in `narrative`. Recommendation: encode in narrative for v1 and require a dedicated `## Ultimate Goal` section in command prompt/output.
- Overlap decision: trust model joint; blast radius duplicate Epics or accidental merge of distinct initiatives; alternatives recommended default vs neutral evidence. User chose neutral evidence with explicit choice.
- Initial entries: trust model joint; blast radius blocking valid early initiatives; alternatives required vs optional. User chose optional entries.

### Draft Spec Deltas
- `rq-epicCreateCommand01` — `/adv-epic` command requires goal-first creation workflow.
  - Given a user invokes `/adv-epic` to create an initiative,
  - When the command prepares an Epic creation plan,
  - Then it requires an explicit ultimate goal, narrative context, scope kind, overlap scan, and user confirmation before calling typed Epic mutation tools.
- `rq-epicCreateCommand01.2` — overlapping Epic decision is user-confirmed.
  - Given an overlap scan finds one or more plausible existing open Epics,
  - When `/adv-epic` presents the creation plan,
  - Then it asks the user to choose update/clarify existing or create new, and does not create a duplicate without that choice.
- `rq-epicCreateCommand01.3` — initial entries are optional.
  - Given the user has a valid ultimate goal and narrative but no initial child work,
  - When `/adv-epic` creates the Epic,
  - Then the Epic may be created with zero initial entries, and shell/link additions remain available later.

### Related Pattern Scan
- Similar command-file pattern: `.opencode/command/adv-idea.md` is collaborative, no-gate, no-change/task creation, and a good structural template.
- Similar overlap behavior: `.opencode/command/adv-proposal.md` already uses `adv_change_list` to detect overlaps and ask only when ambiguity remains.
- Drift anchors: `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts`, `plugin/src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts`, `plugin/src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts`, and `plugin/src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts` should be expanded.

### Completeness Verification
- Problem completeness: high confidence. The problem is not only missing file `adv-epic.md`; it includes absent user-facing workflow, absent ultimate-goal checkpoint, absent overlap decision, and absent command registration/docs/test anchors.
- Solution scope: high confidence. Full intended solution is command contract + manifest/docs/tests + spec requirement; not CLI mutation or Epic schema field change.
- Sole-entry claim: no cross-cutting sole-entry implementation claim is needed. `/adv-epic` is a new entrypoint; existing typed Epic tools remain mutation authority.
- Secondary surfaces: README, SETUP, CLI matrix, manifest-doc drift, and Epic asset tests are in scope. `bin/adv epic create` is out of scope because CLI mutation is explicitly avoided.

### LBP Check
- Recommended direction is long-term best practice: add a boring command contract using existing typed Epic tools and drift tests.
- External-solution check skipped: this is an internal workflow/command surface, not a library/service selection.
- Opportunity scout auto-adopted: encode ultimate goal in narrative for v1, clone `adv-idea` command structure, reuse proposal overlap recipe.
- Opportunity scout deferred: separate structural `ultimate_goal` schema field unless future evidence shows narrative is insufficient.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS — no blocking ambiguity findings. Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C
- Advisory: archived-change related-work scan timed out; current Epic list, active changes, agenda, backlog, specs, and code/test scans provide enough discovery evidence for agreement.

## Recommended Objectives
1. Provide `/adv-epic` as a collaborative no-gate command for creating ADV Epics.
2. Require explicit ultimate goal confirmation before Epic mutation.
3. Require related open work scan before create/update choice.
4. Use existing typed Epic tools for all mutations.
5. Add tests/docs/spec anchors so command behavior does not drift.

## User Decisions
- Create timing: create the Epic after final confirmation when no overlap blocks creation.
- Initial entries: optional; a valid Epic can start with only goal and narrative.
- Overlap default: present evidence neutrally; do not force a default recommendation.

## Remaining Discovery Questions
None requiring user input before agreement. Technical details move to design.
documents.problemStatement: ADV has typed Epic tools, but no user-facing `/adv-epic` command contract. Users need a guided Epic-creation workflow that forces an explicit ultimate goal, checks for related/open overlapping Epics or changes, and asks whether to update an existing Epic or create a new one before mutating state.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement: Add epic command

## Objectives
1. Add `/adv-epic` as a collaborative, no-gate ADV command for creating Epics.
2. Require explicit user-confirmed ultimate goal before any Epic mutation.
3. Require related open work scan before the command creates or updates an Epic.
4. Preserve existing Epic invariants: optional membership, one Epic per change in v1, advisory order, product scope support, and no Jira-like planning fields.
5. Anchor the command in manifest, docs/specs, and tests so behavior does not drift.

## Success Criteria
- SC1. Users can invoke `/adv-epic` to create an Epic through one collaborative, goal-first workflow.
- SC2. Epic creation never proceeds until the user has confirmed the Epic's ultimate goal.
- SC3. Overlapping open Epics/changes/backlog work are surfaced before mutation, with neutral evidence and explicit user choice.
- SC4. Valid Epics can be created with zero initial roadmap entries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC1. `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` exists with frontmatter `name: adv-epic`, exact manifest-matching description, and `requiresChangeId: false`.
- AC2. `plugin/src/manifest.ts` registers `adv-epic` as no-gate, `requiresChangeId: false`, `phase: "utility"` or equivalent no-gate initiative-planning phase.
- AC3. `/adv-epic` command contract requires an explicit `Ultimate Goal` before Epic creation mutation may run.
- AC4. `/adv-epic` command contract requires related-work checks using Epic/change/backlog reads before creation.
- AC5. If plausible overlap exists, command asks user to choose update/clarify existing vs create new before mutation.
- AC6. Command contract states initial shell/change entries are optional; shell/link additions use typed Epic tools only.
- AC7. `advance-epics` spec gains a requirement covering `/adv-epic` goal-first creation and overlap decision behavior. [warrant: spec:rq-epicEntity01]
- AC8. Tests fail without command/manifest/docs/spec anchors and pass after implementation.

## Constraints
- C1. Do not add `bin/adv epic create` or any CLI mutation verb.
- C2. Do not add a separate structural `ultimate_goal` field to the Epic schema in this change; encode the ultimate goal in the command-required narrative structure.
- C3. Do not make initial roadmap entries mandatory.
- C4. Do not make Epic membership mandatory for all ADV changes.
- C5. Do not treat Epic order as a gate/task blocker.
- C6. Do not bypass typed Epic MCP tools or read ADV state files directly.
- C7. Keep overlap detection evidence-backed: typed Epic/change/backlog reads plus user confirmation; heuristics may only rank/summarize evidence.

## Out of Scope
- OOS1. CLI mutation command such as `bin/adv epic create`.
- OOS2. Epic schema migration for a new `ultimate_goal` field.
- OOS3. Mandatory shell/change entries at Epic creation time.
- OOS4. Jira-like assignee, estimate, sprint, board, or ownership workflows.
- OOS5. Automatic implementation change/task creation from every shell entry.
- OOS6. Fixing the pre-existing Epic tool warrant-surface omission; tracked separately as agenda `ag-Adj91lPD`.

## Avoidances
- DONT1. Do not create a new Epic when a clearly overlapping open Epic exists without asking the user to choose update/clarify existing vs create new.
- DONT2. Do not present neutral overlap evidence as a hidden recommendation; user chose neutral evidence, not a default recommendation.
- DONT3. Do not weaken existing product/cross-project Epic guidance.
- DONT4. Do not complete discovery based on archived-change scan success; archived scan degraded due `adv_change_list includeArchived:true` timeout and is not required for this scope.

## Preview Applicability
visual_surface: false

Rationale: This change adds command/spec/test/docs behavior for an agent workflow. It does not affect browser-visible UI or visual output.

## Decisions

### User Decisions
- Create timing: when no overlap blocks creation, `/adv-epic` creates the Epic after one final confirmation.
- Initial entries: optional; an Epic may start with only ultimate goal and narrative.
- Overlap default: present evidence neutrally; do not force a default recommendation.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)
- Use existing `adv_epic_create.narrative` to carry the ultimate goal structure for v1 instead of adding a new schema field.
- Model `/adv-epic` after the collaborative, no-gate structure of `/adv-idea`, but allow typed Epic mutation after user confirmation.
- Reuse `/adv-proposal`'s overlap recipe concept: scan related work, reuse/update obvious overlap, ask only when ambiguity or user choice matters.
- Register `/adv-epic` as `agent-workflow-only` in CLI surface matrix; CLI Epic namespace remains read-only for list.
- Discovery found Epic tool warrants currently do not resolve through `adv_contract_mint` because `getToolSurface()` omits `epicTools`; this is tracked separately as agenda `ag-Adj91lPD`, so this contract avoids tool warrant tags for Epic tools.

## Deferred Questions
None.

## Sign-Off
Criteria approved by user in chat: `approve`.
documents.design:
# Design: Add epic command

## Architecture Overview

Add `/adv-epic` as a command-file workflow over the existing typed Epic MCP tools. The command becomes the user-facing orchestration layer for Epic creation; the existing Epic tools remain the mutation authority.

The design intentionally avoids a new Epic schema field for `ultimate_goal`. Instead, `/adv-epic` requires an explicit `## Ultimate Goal` section in the command workflow and encodes that goal inside the existing required `narrative` passed to `adv_epic_create`. This preserves v1 compatibility and avoids field sprawl while still making the goal mandatory at the command boundary.

## Key Decisions

1. **Command-only user workflow, typed-tool mutation.**
   - `/adv-epic` owns collaboration, overlap evidence, and final confirmation.
   - `adv_epic_create`, `adv_epic_update`, `adv_epic_add_shell`, and `adv_epic_link_change` own state mutation.
   - No direct ADV state reads/writes.

2. **Use `phase: "pre-implementation"`, not a new or utility-only phase.**
   - Existing no-gate idea/problem flows live in pre-implementation.
   - Epic creation is initiative planning before change execution, so pre-implementation is the closest existing phase.
   - Agreement allowed `phase: "utility"` or equivalent no-gate initiative-planning phase; this satisfies the equivalent path.

3. **Do not clone `/adv-idea` read-only boundary.**
   - `/adv-idea` is a structural template for collaborative framing only.
   - `/adv-epic` mutates after final user confirmation, closer to `/adv-proposal` in mutation semantics.
   - Manifest scope must show Epic creation/modification, not empty read-only scope.

4. **Overlap evidence stays neutral.**
   - User chose neutral evidence when overlap exists.
   - The command should present evidence and concrete choices: update/clarify existing Epic, create new Epic, or stop.
   - The command must not hide a recommendation as the default action.

5. **Initial entries are optional.**
   - A valid Epic can be goal+narrative only.
   - Shells and linked changes are optional follow-on steps within the same confirmed flow or later.

6. **Spec law is minimal.**
   - Add one `advance-epics` requirement (`rq-epicCreateCommand01`) for the user-facing command contract.
   - Do not broaden the Epic data model or CLI namespace.

## ADR Drafts

No ADR draft required. The decision is not hard to reverse, surprising, and tradeoff-heavy enough to warrant a standalone ADR. Existing spec + command docs provide sufficient context.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Create command contract**
   - Add `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md`.
   - Frontmatter:
     - `name: adv-epic`
     - description exactly matching manifest, likely `Create Epics with goal-first workflow`.
   - Manifest comment: `requiresChangeId: false`; no gate.
   - Command phases:
     1. Parse user request and load `advance-epics` spec context.
     2. Elicit/confirm title, ultimate goal, narrative context, scope kind, constraints, and optional initial entries.
     3. Run related-work scan: `adv_epic_list`; `adv_epic_show` for plausible active Epics; `adv_change_list`; `adv_backlog_state` when backlog/roadmap relevance is plausible.
     4. Present overlap evidence neutrally. If plausible overlap exists, ask user to choose update/clarify existing, create new, or stop.
     5. Present final plan and require explicit final confirmation.
     6. On confirmation, call typed tools: `adv_epic_create`; then optional `adv_epic_add_shell` / `adv_epic_link_change` only for user-approved entries.
     7. Report created/updated Epic ID, entries added/linked, skipped items, and retry/repair path for partial failures.

2. **Register command**
   - Add `adv-epic` to `plugin/src/manifest.ts`.
   - Use `phase: "pre-implementation"`, `requiresChangeId: false`, no gate, no prerequisites.
   - Successors should include `adv-proposal`, and optionally `adv-roadmap`/`adv-status` only if existing navigation patterns support them. Prefer minimal successor set to avoid misleading flow.
   - Scope should show `creates: ["epic"]`, reads `specs`, `codebase`/`changes`/`backlog` as appropriate, and modifies `epic`/membership projections through typed tools.

3. **Add spec requirement**
   - Update `.adv/specs/advance-epics/spec.json` with `rq-epicCreateCommand01`.
   - Mirror in `docs/specs/advance-epics.md`.
   - Requirement covers: explicit ultimate goal, related-work scan, overlap decision before duplicate creation, final confirmation, typed-tool mutations, optional initial entries.

4. **Add drift/test anchors**
   - `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts`: add `adv-epic`, update expectedCommands array, update test title string from 26 to 27, and update `toHaveLength(26)` to 27.
   - `plugin/src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts`: existing test should catch frontmatter/manifest mismatch automatically.
   - `plugin/src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts`: existing test should require a row after command file exists.
   - `plugin/src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts`: add command-contract tests for `Ultimate Goal`, overlap behavior, neutral choice, typed tools, optional entries, and `rq-epicCreateCommand01` spec/doc mirror.
   - Add or update any README/SETUP command table tests if present.

5. **Update docs/config surfaces**
   - README command reference.
   - SETUP command table.
   - `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md` row with `agent-workflow-only` disposition.
   - `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` command list/routing if it has a command-only list entry.
   - `token-budgets.json` if it enumerates per-command budgets.

## LBP Analysis

The long-term best path is a boring command workflow over existing typed tools.

- It keeps correctness structural: schema/tool state remains owned by Epic tools, while command behavior is enforced by spec/doc/asset tests.
- It avoids a premature `ultimate_goal` schema migration. The command can force a goal section in narrative now; a future schema field remains possible if usage proves narrative insufficient.
- It avoids CLI mutation, preserving the current read-only `adv epic list --json` contract.
- It reuses existing no-gate command patterns but corrects the boundary: `/adv-epic` mutates only after explicit confirmation.

## Affected Components

- `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md`
- `plugin/src/manifest.ts`
- `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts`
- `plugin/src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts`
- `.adv/specs/advance-epics/spec.json`
- `docs/specs/advance-epics.md`
- `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md`
- `README.md`
- `SETUP.md`
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` if command lists/routing require it
- `.opencode/token-budgets.json` if per-command budget enumeration requires it

## Design-Derived Criteria

- DDC1. Command mutation phase is reachable only after explicit final confirmation text in the command contract.
- DDC2. Command overlap scan must use typed tools; wording may rank or summarize evidence but cannot declare overlap as final authority without user choice.
- DDC3. Manifest/frontmatter description must be a single-line exact match and satisfy existing manifest-doc-drift constraints.
- DDC4. Tests must include a failing assertion specifically tied to `/adv-epic` ultimate-goal and overlap behavior, not only generic command existence.
- DDC5. Spec delta must be mirrored in both JSON spec and markdown spec doc.
- DDC6. Prep must explicitly verify both manifest test count/list and title string updates.

## Risks / Mitigations

- **Risk: accidental duplicate Epic creation.**
  - Mitigation: require typed related-work scan and user choice before creating when overlap appears plausible.

- **Risk: command prompt says read-only while mutating.**
  - Mitigation: explicitly model mutation-after-confirm boundary; manifest scope declares Epic creation/modification.

- **Risk: adding command breaks drift tests.**
  - Mitigation: update manifest count/list/title, command doc frontmatter, CLI surface matrix, README/SETUP, Epic asset tests, and token budget list if needed.

- **Risk: product/cross-project guidance regresses.**
  - Mitigation: command contract must preserve target-path trust and typed membership tools; agenda `Fix Epic cross-project guidance` remains related context.

- **Risk: contract warrant tags for Epic tools fail today.**
  - Mitigation: out of scope for this change; tracked as agenda `ag-Adj91lPD`. Do not rely on tool warrant tags for Epic tools until fixed.

## Design Leverage Scout

- Candidates considered: 5.
- Auto-adopted: 4.
  - Use `phase: "pre-implementation"` rather than inventing a phase.
  - Correct command boundary to mutation-after-confirm, not read-only.
  - Update manifest test count/list/title from 26 to 27.
  - Add explicit `/adv-epic` command-contract asset tests and `rq-epicCreateCommand01` mirror coverage.
- Surfaced/partial: 1.
  - Verify extra drift surfaces during prep: `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` command-only lists and `.opencode/token-budgets.json`.
- Inconclusive/skipped: none.

## Validator Result

Validator: VALIDATED with one caution.

- Correctness: CAUTION — design solves objectives; prep must make manifest.test.ts dual edit explicit: expectedCommands array and `toHaveLength(26)` plus title string.
- Simplicity: VALIDATED — command-over-typed-tools is simplest correct path; schema field, new phase, or CLI mutation would add unjustified complexity.
- Spec-law compliance: VALIDATED — preserves read-only CLI Epic namespace and all Epic invariants.
- Key alternatives: VALIDATED — no significant viable alternative overlooked.

Recommendation: proceed to prep. Keep `phase: "pre-implementation"`, declare mutation boundary honestly in manifest scope, update both manifest test list/count/title, and verify `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` plus `.opencode/token-budgets.json` drift surfaces.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome
Added `/adv-epic` as a goal-first, overlap-aware, no-gate ADV command for creating Epics through typed Epic tools. Acceptance review found no remaining blockers or nonblocking findings after a frontmatter metadata remediation.

## Verdict
APPROVED

## What Was Built
1. Added `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` with explicit Ultimate Goal confirmation, related-work scan, neutral overlap choice, optional initial entries, and typed Epic mutation boundaries.
2. Added `rq-epicCreateCommand01` to the `advance-epics` JSON spec and markdown mirror.
3. Registered `/adv-epic` in the command manifest and public command surfaces: README, SETUP, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS, CLI surface matrix, and token budget baseline.
4. Added/updated tests covering command frontmatter, command contract text, manifest/doc drift, CLI matrix classification, and spec/doc anchors.
5. Remediated acceptance review gap by adding `requiresChangeId: false` to command YAML frontmatter and strengthening the asset test to assert frontmatter metadata exactly.

## What Was Verified
- Verdict: APPROVED / READY; reviewer reported 0 blocking and 0 nonblocking findings after remediation.
- Tests: `pnpm exec vitest run src/advance-epics-assets.test.ts src/manifest.test.ts src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts` passed with 100 tests.
- Schemas: `pnpm run schemas:check` passed.
- Change validation: `adv_change_validate strict:true` passed with only expected `NO_DELTAS` warning.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — agreement declares `visual_surface: false`; delivered work is command/spec/docs/tests only, with no browser-visible surface.
- Contract matrix: 29 required rows passed/respected; 0 failed/violated/unknown.

## Remaining Concerns
None for this change. Separate follow-up remains: agenda `ag-Adj91lPD` tracks Epic tool warrant-surface exposure.

# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-26T18:40:45.000Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Users can invoke `/adv-epic` to create an Epic through one collaborative, goal-first workflow. | pass | `/adv-epic` command file exists and is registered in manifest/docs. Reviewer verdict READY; targeted test suite passed 100 tests. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | Epic creation never proceeds until the user has confirmed the Epic's ultimate goal. | pass | `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` requires explicit Ultimate Goal confirmation before create/update mutation. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | Overlapping open Epics/changes/backlog work are surfaced before mutation, with neutral evidence and explicit user choice. | pass | Command contract requires related-work scan and overlap decision before mutation; asset tests cover overlap handling. |
| SC4 | success_criterion | Valid Epics can be created with zero initial roadmap entries. | pass | Command contract states initial entries are optional and `adv_epic_create` may run without shell/change entries. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` exists with frontmatter `name: adv-epic`, exact manifest-matching description, and `requiresChangeId: false`. | pass | `.opencode/command/adv-epic.md` frontmatter includes `name: adv-epic`, `description: Gather Epic goals before typed creation`, and `requiresChangeId: false`; `advance-epics-assets.test.ts` asserts these; targeted suite passed. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | `plugin/src/manifest.ts` registers `adv-epic` as no-gate, `requiresChangeId: false`, `phase: "utility"` or equivalent no-gate initiative-planning phase. | pass | `plugin/src/manifest.ts` registers `adv-epic` with `requiresChangeId:false`, no gate, and `phase:"pre-implementation"`; manifest tests passed. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | `/adv-epic` command contract requires an explicit `Ultimate Goal` before Epic creation mutation may run. | pass | Command contract includes explicit Ultimate Goal checkpoint before any Epic mutation; asset tests passed. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | `/adv-epic` command contract requires related-work checks using Epic/change/backlog reads before creation. | pass | Command contract requires `adv_epic_list`/`adv_epic_show`, `adv_change_list`, and backlog read before creation; asset tests passed. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | If plausible overlap exists, command asks user to choose update/clarify existing vs create new before mutation. | pass | Command contract requires user choice to update/clarify existing vs create new when plausible overlap exists; asset tests passed. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | Command contract states initial shell/change entries are optional; shell/link additions use typed Epic tools only. | pass | Command contract states initial shell/change entries are optional and shell/link additions use typed Epic tools only; asset tests passed. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | `advance-epics` spec gains a requirement covering `/adv-epic` goal-first creation and overlap decision behavior. | pass | `advance-epics` spec and markdown mirror add `rq-epicCreateCommand01`; spec/docs asset tests and schemas check passed. |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | Tests fail without command/manifest/docs/spec anchors and pass after implementation. | pass | RED/GREEN task evidence recorded for missing command/manifest/docs rows; final targeted suite passed 100 tests after implementation/remediation. |
| C1 | constraint | Do not add `bin/adv epic create` or any CLI mutation verb. | respected | No `bin/adv epic create` or CLI mutation added; CLI surface matrix row is `agent-workflow-only`; CLI matrix test passed. |
| C2 | constraint | Do not add a separate structural `ultimate_goal` field to the Epic schema in this change; encode the ultimate goal in the command-required narrative structure. | respected | No Epic schema field `ultimate_goal` added; goal is encoded in command-required narrative structure. |
| C3 | constraint | Do not make initial roadmap entries mandatory. | respected | Command contract explicitly allows creating Epic with zero initial entries. |
| C4 | constraint | Do not make Epic membership mandatory for all ADV changes. | respected | No change made requiring Epic membership for all ADV changes; existing optional-membership guidance preserved. |
| C5 | constraint | Do not treat Epic order as a gate/task blocker. | respected | No command/spec/doc language makes Epic order a gate or task blocker; existing advisory-order guidance preserved. |
| C6 | constraint | Do not bypass typed Epic MCP tools or read ADV state files directly. | respected | Command contract uses typed Epic MCP tools for mutations and typed reads for state; no direct ADV state file reads introduced. |
| C7 | constraint | Keep overlap detection evidence-backed: typed Epic/change/backlog reads plus user confirmation; heuristics may only rank/summarize evidence. | respected | Overlap scan is evidence-backed via typed Epic/change/backlog reads; command text treats heuristics as neutral summarization/ranking only. |
| OOS1 | out_of_scope | CLI mutation command such as `bin/adv epic create`. | respected | No CLI mutation command added; CLI surface matrix remains agent-workflow-only for `/adv-epic`. |
| OOS2 | out_of_scope | Epic schema migration for a new `ultimate_goal` field. | respected | No Epic schema migration for `ultimate_goal`. |
| OOS3 | out_of_scope | Mandatory shell/change entries at Epic creation time. | respected | Command allows zero initial entries and does not require shell/change entries. |
| OOS4 | out_of_scope | Jira-like assignee, estimate, sprint, board, or ownership workflows. | respected | No assignee/estimate/sprint/board/ownership workflow fields or docs added. |
| OOS5 | out_of_scope | Automatic implementation change/task creation from every shell entry. | respected | No automatic implementation change/task creation from shell entries added. |
| OOS6 | out_of_scope | Fixing the pre-existing Epic tool warrant-surface omission; tracked separately as agenda `ag-Adj91lPD`. | respected | Epic tool warrant-surface omission remains tracked separately as agenda `ag-Adj91lPD`; this change avoided requiring that fix. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not create a new Epic when a clearly overlapping open Epic exists without asking the user to choose update/clarify existing vs create new. | respected | Command contract requires overlap decision before creating a new Epic when plausible existing open work overlaps. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not present neutral overlap evidence as a hidden recommendation; user chose neutral evidence, not a default recommendation. | respected | Command contract presents overlap evidence neutrally and asks user to choose; no default recommendation encoded. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | Do not weaken existing product/cross-project Epic guidance. | respected | Product/cross-project Epic guidance in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS remains present; `/adv-epic` row only adds command surface. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | Do not complete discovery based on archived-change scan success; archived scan degraded due `adv_change_list includeArchived:true` timeout and is not required for this scope. | respected | Discovery degraded archived-change scan was not used as completion proof; implementation scope relies on active typed reads/command contract/tests. |

clarify_findings.message: Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
